titleStmt.title: Letter from George Hugh Chetwood Townsend to Lady Clonbrock, 1 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: George Hugh Chetwood Townsend

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from George Hugh Chetwood Townsend (b. 1872) to Lady Clonbrock (1840-1928). The letter was addressed to John James Thompson (1872-1924), the registrar at the Royal Hospital for Incurables. George Townsend was writing on behalf of the Tipperary Wounded Soldiers' Fund. The letter thanks Clonbrock for her donation to the fund and for allowing her name to be used in connection with the association.Augusta Caroline Dillon (née Crofton) was the wife of Luke Gerald Dillon (1834-1917), the 4th Baron of Clonbrock, Co. Galway and the daughter of Lord Crofton of Mote Park (Edward Henry Churchill Crofton, 3rd Baron), Co. Roscommon. Aged 75 at the outbreak of war, Lady Clonbrock, worked closely with the Irish Women's Association to send basic necessities to Irish POWs. Many of her care packages went to members of the Connaught Rangers imprisoned in Limburg near Cologne.

msIdentifier.collection: Clonbrock Estate Papers, Collection List No. 54, Ms. 35,796/12/2

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Cordangan Manor' Tipperary. 1st April 1916. My Lady, It is very good of you to send a donation to our
Wounded Soldiers' Fund; and also to allow your name to be
used in connection with the Association that has been formed
to further help the cause of these men. I feel that you also
have to be thanked for the donation sent by the Committee of the
Connaught Rangers War Fund, which has just been received from
your Treasurer. I hope to be able to further write to you
before long about this work. Yours faithfully, George C. Townsend The Lady Clonbrock, Clonbrock, Ahaskeragh, Ireland.
1916
April 3
Mr Townsend
Tipperary Wounded
Soldiers
